commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed after contributor proof and the URL-validation fixes; the missing real behavior proof makes this a human-only merge gate rather than a repair-only lane.; Needs attention: The patch introduces an automatic browser launch path that should validate recovery URLs before using a persistent profile.; Review finding: [P2] Validate recovery URLs before relaunching Chrome — src/browser/recoverConversat... (sha=80bf6996cf5c06e89ab787801c3102f109872b8b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds default-on `oracle session <id> --harvest/--live` recovery by relaunching Chrome from saved session metadata, plus `--no-recover`, tests, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking findings: source inspection shows the recovery selector accepts any `http` candidate and prefers `runtime.tabUrl`, while current main's recoverability helper rejects non-conversation ChatGPT shell URLs and external URLs. I did not run browser smokes in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed after contributor proof and the URL-validation fixes; the missing real behavior proof makes this a human-only merge gate rather than a repair-only lane.

target.title: feat(session): recover --harvest/--live by reopening saved tab URL
target.body:
## Summary

- When `oracle session <id> --harvest` or `--live` fails to find a live ChatGPT tab, automatically relaunch Chrome with the session's manual-login profile, navigate to the conversation URL stored in session metadata, and retry the harvest against the recovered tab.
- Default on; opt out with `--no-recover` on the `session` subcommand.
- New module `src/browser/recoverConversation.ts` resolves the URL from `meta.browser.runtime.tabUrl` (falling back to `meta.browser.harvest.url`) and the profile dir from `meta.browser.config.manualLoginProfileDir` (falling back to `defaultManualLoginProfileDir()`), then uses `chrome-launcher` to spin up Chrome with the profile and URL.
- Recovered Chrome stays open after harvest so the user can read the conversation; `closeAfterRecover: true` (internal option) closes it.
- Tests in `tests/cli/browserTabsRecover.test.ts` cover the recovery path, the opt-out, and the close-after-recover behavior.

## Why

Long GPT-5.x Pro Extended runs frequently complete in the background after the CLI's 20-minute wall expires. The original session has the full conversation URL persisted in `~/.oracle/sessions/<id>/meta.json` (`browser.runtime.tabUrl`), and ChatGPT preserves attachments and the assistant response at that URL, but `--harvest` and `--live` only look at *live* tabs in a running Chrome — when the original CLI exits, the browser closes and the response becomes unrecoverable through `oracle`.

The motivating concrete case: a 191k-token GPT-5.5 Pro Extended code review timed out at the 20-minute wall. The original CLI saved `meta.browser.runtime.tabUrl` but exited with only "I" captured. The actual 17.5KB review had completed in ChatGPT's background. With this patch, `oracle session <slug> --live --write-output ...` relaunches the profile, reopens the URL, harvests the now-complete response, and writes it to disk in ~10 seconds.

## Verification

- `pnpm run check` — clean (typecheck + format + lint)
- `pnpm docs:check` — ok (85 flags, 6 files)
- `pnpm vitest run` — 1066/1066 pass
- Manual end-to-end against the timed-out session described above: full 17.5KB markdown response recovered.

## Notes

- This addresses the same family of issues as #210 / #211 (Pro Extended detection / session disconnect handling) by adding a recovery path after the assistant response actually completes, rather than improving detection during the run.
- The recovery launch uses a minimal flag set (anti-detection + lang + no-first-run + use-mock-keychain) — kept narrow on purpose to avoid drifting from `launchChrome`'s production flag set in `chromeLifecycle.ts`. Happy to refactor to share a helper if you'd prefer one source of truth.
- No CLI flag was added for `closeAfterRecover` (kept it as an internal option); could expose `--close-after-recover` if useful.
target.author: hbruceweaver
